title: notelaea pungens
intro: Notelaea pungens, also known as the Tasmanian pepper tree, is a species of flowering plant in the family Apocynaceae. It is native to the coastal regions of Tasmania, Australia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, making it useful for treating wounds and skin conditions. The leaves of the plant can be made into herbal preparations, such as teas and tinctures, which have been used to aid digestion and relieve respiratory issues.
